categories.name: windsurf
categories.description: Windsurf IDE documentation — MCP integration, Rules/Memories/AGENTS.md, Cascade Hooks, Workflows/Skills, and Windsurf-Next platform compatibility. Updated from Windsurf University and docs.windsurf.com (Wave 14+, Windsurf 2.0 era).

documents.name: mcp_overview
documents.description: Overview of MCP (Model Context Protocol) integration in Windsurf Cascade. Includes OAuth support, one-click deeplink install, and key facts for extension authors.

documents.name: mcp_config
documents.description: How to configure MCP servers in Windsurf using mcp_config.json, including popular server examples and one-click install via deeplink

documents.description: Admin controls for Teams and Enterprises including MCP registry, server whitelisting, regex pattern matching, and MCP Whitelist

documents.name: memories_rules
documents.description: Windsurf Memories, Rules, AGENTS.md, and activation modes (always_on, glob, model_decision, manual). Covers scope levels, storage locations, character limits, and best practices.

documents.name: cascade_hooks
documents.description: Cascade Hooks — shell commands triggered at key workflow points (pre/post MCP tool use, write code, run command, user prompt, cascade response). Introduced Wave 13. Exit code 2 blocks action.

documents.name: workflows_skills
documents.description: Windsurf Workflows (slash-command triggered multi-step procedures), Skills (progressive-disclosure bundles with supporting files), and AGENTS.md (location-scoped always-on/glob rules).

documents.name: platform_compat
documents.description: Windsurf-Next vs VS Code API surface truth: FULL/PARTIAL/BLOCKED/REPLACED/STUBBED API matrix, tool schema compliance rules, known name conflicts, compliance checklist, and current model list.

instructions.addingDocuments: Place IDE and MCP documentation in the appropriate subfolder. Update this index.json with document metadata.
instructions.downloadingSources: Download from official documentation sites including docs.windsurf.com, modelcontextprotocol.io, code.visualstudio.com, and docs.continue.dev.
instructions.namingConvention: Use clear filenames like: mcp_config.md, server_concepts.md, extension_api_overview.md
instructions.licenseCompliance: Include license information for each document. Most official docs are MIT or proprietary.
instructions.guardRails: When retrieving documentation, prioritize MCP server and Windsurf-Next specific content. Use this corpus when answering questions about IDE extensions, MCP protocol, Windsurf configuration, VS Code extension development, backend engineering patterns, database design, or performance optimization.
